prompt: What do you personally do right after a meet—jump into the next block immediately or take time off?
expected_output: The answer is context-dependent. If an athlete is motivated and recovered, they can return quickly; if beat up, use an easier first week with roughly 70-80% loading and reduced volume. They frame complete time off as acceptable when needed, since one week in a year is trivial. Their preference is usually to keep intensity present while temporarily pulling back volume.
assertions: Answer captures the core SBS position on post-competition transition week
Answer includes key specific details, numbers, or mechanisms mentioned by the hosts
Answer acknowledges important caveats or limitations discussed by the hosts
Answer does not contradict the hosts' stated position
